title: Running Python Code
summary: Learn how to execute Python code using the ZoeSkoul browser code editor, Run button, and output panel.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Running Python Code
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Running Python code is a fundamental skill for any programmer. In this lesson, you'll learn how to use the ZoeSkoul browser code editor to write and execute Python programs. The process involves typing your code in the editor, clicking the Run button, and observing the results in the output panel. This setup allows you to quickly test and debug your code without needing any additional software.

### Try it yourself
Type `print("Hello, World!")` in the code editor and click Run. Check the output panel to see the result.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding the Output
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you run a Python program, the output panel displays the results of your code. For example, if you write:

```
print("Hello, World!")
```

The output panel will show:

```
Hello, World!
```

This is because the `print` function sends the specified message to the output panel. Understanding how to interpret this output is crucial for debugging and verifying your code.

### Your turn
Modify the code to print your name instead of "Hello, World!" and run it again.

quizDraft.title: Purpose of the Run Button
quizDraft.prompt: What is the purpose of the Run button in the ZoeSkoul browser code editor?
quizDraft.hint: Think about what happens when you click Run after writing code.

help.concept: The Run button executes the code written in the editor.
help.hint_1: The Run button is used to execute the code.
help.hint_2: Clicking Run processes the code and shows the output.

quizDraft.title: Components of the ZoeSkoul Workspace
quizDraft.prompt: Which components are part of the ZoeSkoul browser workspace for running Python code?
quizDraft.hint: Consider the tools you use to write, run, and view the output of your code.

help.concept: The workspace includes tools for writing, running, and viewing code output.
help.hint_1: Think about where you write code and where you see the results.
help.hint_2: The workspace includes an editor, a Run button, and an output panel.

quizDraft.title: Steps to Run Python Code
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the steps to run Python code in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Start with writing the code and end with checking the output.

help.concept: Running code involves writing, executing, and checking the output.
help.hint_1: Begin with writing the code in the editor.
help.hint_2: Finish by checking the output panel.

tokens: Write code in the code editor
correctOrder: Write code in the code editor

quizDraft.title: Order of Execution
quizDraft.prompt: Put the following actions in the order they occur when running a Python program.
quizDraft.hint: Think about the sequence from writing to seeing the output.

help.concept: The sequence involves writing, running, and viewing results.

tokens: View results in the output panel
correctOrder: View results in the output panel

quizDraft.title: Function to Display Output
quizDraft.prompt: Which function is used to display output in Python?
quizDraft.hint: It's a built-in function that sends text to the output panel.

help.concept: The function sends text to the output panel.

help.hint_2: The function starts with 'p' and ends with 't'.

quizDraft.title: Python Code Execution
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are true about executing Python code in the ZoeSkoul workspace?
quizDraft.hint: Consider the process and tools involved in running code.

help.concept: Executing code involves using the editor, Run button, and output panel.
help.hint_1: Think about the steps from writing to viewing output.

options: You write code in the code editor
You click Run to execute the code
The output appears in the terminal
The output appears in the output panel

quizDraft.title: Role of the Code Editor
quizDraft.prompt: What is the role of the code editor in the ZoeSkoul workspace?
quizDraft.hint: Think about where you write your Python code.

help.concept: The code editor is where you write your Python code.
help.hint_1: It's the place for typing your code.
help.hint_2: The editor is used to write and edit code.

quizDraft.title: Simple Python Program
quizDraft.prompt: Write a Python program that reads a name from input and prints a greeting message.
quizDraft.hint: Use the input function to read a name and print to display the message.

help.concept: Use input to read data and print to display it.
help.hint_1: Use input() to get the name.
help.hint_2: Use print() to show the greeting.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# Read a name from input
name = input("Enter your name: ")

# Print a greeting message
quizDraft.solutionCode:
# Read a name from input
name = input("Enter your name: ")

# Print a greeting message
print("Hello, " + name + "!")
